preview_text:
The statistical model favors Belgium with a 54% win probability, substantially above Egypt's 17% chance. The gap reflects a considerable quality differential in the forecast, though the underdog retains a non-trivial path to victory.

The draw emerges as the second-likeliest outcome at 29%, suggesting the match will be competitive rather than one-sided. Belgium's advantage is real but not overwhelming; nearly three in ten neutral-venue contests between these sides would end level according to the model. The spread across outcomes indicates a match with genuine uncertainty beneath the surface favourite.

The sportsbook consensus aligns closely with the model, pricing Belgium at 58% and drawing the same 24% draw probability. Both sources largely agree on Egypt's 17-18% floor. Polymarket prices show material divergence, assigning equal odds to all three outcomes; this reflects either different underlying assessments or lower liquidity in that market. The dashboard makes no claim about which source is most reliable.
